ecosystemflora:overview-text: Ecosystem - Flora Wild plants spread, compete, follow seasons, and can die from niche stress. Every block stays vanilla — only behaviour changes. Spread and competition Mature plants try to occupy nearby cells. A stronger species can displace a weaker one. Plants in the wrong place accumulate stress and die; ferns and many berry bushes need a host tree nearby. Ecology inspect — I Aim at a wild plant, tree trunk, mushroom, or mycelium soil for a live report: registry status, stress, next spread, season, mat edge (reeds/lilies), tree age and size, mycelium network. Details on the Ecology Inspect page. Trees Mature trunks scatter saplings; optional yearly aging and death at species lifespan. Calendar age is saved with the world. See Trees and Seasonal Canopy. Scope Ecology runs on plants in loaded chunks. Wild spread, aging, and senescence do not run inside land claims. Config ModConfig/ecosystemflora.json — presets natural, lush, sparse. See Configuration Guide.
ecosystemflora:flowers-text: Meadow Flowers (21 species) Flowers form the backbone of meadow ecology. They compete for open ground and respond to climate and soil. Fast colonizers (spread rate 1.7–2.8, weak hold) • Horsetail — wide climate tolerance, very fast, displaced by climax species • Heather, Western Gorse — open meadows, poor soils; soil depleters (slightly dry soil while living) • Red Top Grass — temperate open meadows • Mugwort — warm climates, quick colonizer • Cowparsley — moderate colonizer, meadows and edges • Lupine — nitrogen fixer, improves soil over time Steady meadow (spread rate 1.0–1.6, moderate hold) • Catmint — partial shade to open, edges • Cornflower — dry to mesic meadows, classic wildflower • Wild Daisy — open meadows, moderate hold • Forget-me-not — moist meadows • Woad — dry tolerant, wide climate • Bluebell — forest specialist, needs canopy Slow / localized (spread rate 0.3–0.65, strong hold) • Daffodil — warm, holds well once established • Ghost Pipe (white, pink, red) — cold forests, very slow • Orange Mallow — hot and dry, rare • Edelweiss — cold mountain edge, rare • Golden Poppy — narrow hot climate, very rare Forest species Bluebell, Lily of the Valley, Ghost Pipes thrive in forest interior with strong hold and symbiosis dependency on nearby trees. Harvest Empty hand → flower block. Knife or scythe → drygrass (scythe mows all meadow flowers).
ecosystemflora:ferns-text: Ferns (5 species) Ferns occupy the forest edge and understory. They prefer shade, moisture, and depend on nearby trees for survival. Species • Eagle Fern — classic edge fern, strong context bonus (x2.5), spread rate 1.4 • Cinnamon Fern — forest edge, moderate speed • Deer Fern — similar to cinnamon fern • Hart's Tongue — slower, prefers deep shade • Tall Fern — 2 blocks tall, forest edge Behavior • Context affinity: Edge — bonus near forest borders • Symbiosis: depend on nearby trees (log-grown within 2–4 blocks) • Niche: prefer Shade to Partial light, Mesic to Wet moisture • When their host tree is cut, ferns accumulate stress and die • Minimum sunlight requirement: 7 (under canopy)
ecosystemflora:trees-text: Trees (14 species) Sapling spread A mature log-grown trunk can place a free sapling on open soil nearby when light is sufficient. From sapling to adult, vanilla treegen handles growth — the mod only sets the sapling. Between November and February trees do not spread. Press I on any log: climate and soil are taken from the trunk base, not from mid-air blocks. Aging (EnableTreeAging, on by default) Each game year in loaded chunks, registered trunks gain one calendar year and may add a log, branchy block, or leaves unless already full-sized. Age starts at 0 when the tree enters ecology — a tall worldgen tree can be age 0. Age is stored in the save and restored when the chunk rescans the trunk after a restart. Senescence (EnableTreeSenescence, on by default) Each species has a lifespan (oak about 120 years, birch less). When age reaches it, the next yearly pass removes the whole tree without item drops. Inside a land claim removal is blocked. Species: oak, pine, birch, maple, acacia, kapok, walnut, larch, redwood, ebony, purpleheart, baldcypress, mangrove, joshua. Bamboo and aged wood are not included. Trunks do not die from stress — only from senescence when enabled.
ecosystemflora:berries-text: Berry Bushes (10 wild species) Wild berry bushes spread slowly and prefer forest edges with appropriate soil. Species • Blueberry, Blackberry, Black Currant, Red Currant, White Currant • Cranberry, Strawberry, Beautyberry, Cloudberry, Lingonberry Behavior • Context affinity: mostly Edge (bonus at forest borders) • Soil: prefer forest floor and medium-fertility soils • LocalForestCover: need nearby trees for optimal fitness • Slow spread rate (0.5–0.8), strong hold once established • Strawberry is the exception: open meadow, fast but weak hold Soil succession Berry bushes contribute to soil enrichment over their lifetime. When they die naturally, they improve the soil tier beneath them.
ecosystemflora:aquatic-text: Aquatic Flora (5 species) Water plants have unique placement rules tied to substrate and water depth. Spread models • Reeds (cattail, tule, papyrus): rhizome mat — only stems on the edge of a stand spread one shore block at a time. Rare seed/fragment jumps (~6–10% of attempts) can land farther along the bank. • Water lily: floating pad mat — edge pads fill adjacent open water (including diagonals). Rare seed jumps (~5%) across open water. • Water crowfoot: classic radius spread on underwater gravel columns. • Press I (inspect) on any plant to see mat edge (frontier) and seed chance. Cooper's Reed (Cattail) • Shore: on muddygravel or gravel, water within 3 blocks (land-normal variant) • Shallow water: exactly 1 water block above gravel bed (water-normal variant) • No spread into deep water (2+ blocks) • No vertical stacking in same column • Harvested as one plant: stems first, then roots (vanilla) Tule (Bulrush) • Same placement rules as Cooper's Reed • Temperate climates (5–25°C) • Slightly slower rhizome spread than cattail Papyrus • Similar to reed but prefers warm climates (24–40°C) • 2 blocks tall • Exactly 1 water layer above substrate Water Lily • Spreads as a floating pad mat on open water surfaces • Needs still water, no current obstructions Water Crowfoot • Underwater column plant: section blocks with tip/top (~35% flowering) • Water depth: 2–8 blocks above substrate • Registry tracks the column base block • Substrate: gravel or muddygravel
ecosystemflora:canopy-text: Seasonal canopy Deciduous log-grown trees — oak, birch, maple, and others — change foliage by season. Conifers are unchanged. Autumn — branchy crown thins unevenly; colour shows through the skeleton. Winter — bare branchy frame can remain. Spring — buds on bark, branchy refills, normal leaves return. Runs in loaded chunks; state catches up when you visit. Optional leaf particles near tall crowns (EnableCanopyAmbience). Seasonal foliage and tree aging are separate: this page is crown dress by season; calendar growth and senescence are on Trees. Disable with EnableSeasonalFoliage for static vanilla crowns.
ecosystemflora:inspect-text: Ecology inspect Hotkey: I (configurable). Server-side report for the block you aim at. Valid targets Meadow flowers and grass, ferns, berries, aquatic plants, any log-grown on a wild tree (one report per tree), mushroom caps and mycelium on forest floor, soil, or peat. Typical lines Registry status, stress, next spread attempt, seasonal multiplier, niche fit, symbiosis, climate survival. Reeds/lilies: spread mode, mat edge, seed chance. Trees: calendar age vs lifespan, trunk/crown size %. Mycelium: network edge, anchor stress. Trees Age counts from ecology registration, not worldgen. Large worldgen trees can show full size at age 0. Soil and climate always from the trunk base. Disabled inside land claims. Cooldown and scan radius: EnableEcologyInspect and related keys in config.
ecosystemflora:tuning-text: Configuration Guide File: ModConfig/ecosystemflora.json. Missing keys are filled in when the mod loads. Presets natural (default) — ~72 spread attempts/year, ~50% success. lush — faster meadows, 120 attempts. sparse — subtle, 36 attempts. custom — manual values only. Main toggles EcosystemEnabled, seasonal ecology, displacement, stress death, symbiosis, land claims, trampling (off), ecology inspect (I). Trees EnableTreeAging, EnableTreeSenescence (both default on). Calendar age persists in the save. Canopy & mycelium EnableSeasonalFoliage, EnableCanopyAmbience, EnableMyceliumEcology, network spread. Soil UseSoilSuccession, EnableFallowRestoration. Performance OnlyActivateNearPlayers defaults to false — ecology on all plants in loaded chunks. Set true only to limit activity to ~192 blocks from players (testing).
ecosystemflora:handbook-season-spread: Seasonal spread: Spring {0}x, Summer {1}x, Fall {2}x, Winter {3}x
ecosystemflora:dominance-climax: Climax (slow to spread, hard to displace)
ecosystemflora:inspect-error-notarget: Aim at a wild plant to inspect.
ecosystemflora:inspect-error-noplant: That block is not part of the ecosystem.
ecosystemflora:inspect-error-disabled: Ecology inspect is disabled in config.
ecosystemflora:inspect-error-claim: Cannot inspect inside a land claim.
ecosystemflora:inspect-error-cooldown: Wait a moment before inspecting again.
ecosystemflora:inspect-error-dialog: Could not open ecology inspect window.
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-not-register: Not in spread registry (vanilla block only).
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-tree-senesce: Senescent — past natural lifespan; will die on next ecology year tick (unless land claim blocks removal).
Approaching senescence — natural death next game year if aging is active.
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-tree-size: Structure: trunk {0} blocks, crown radius {1} ({2}% of typical mature ~{3}/{4}).
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-symbiosis-mi: Symbiosis: no host tree — at risk.
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-no-climate: No climate data at this position.
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-mat-frontier: Mat edge: yes — can spread outward.
Mat edge: no — interior; only rare seed may jump.
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-mycelium-anc: Mycelium anchor underfoot — meadow spread blocked.
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-mycelium-mea: Near mycelium zone ({0} blocks): meadow spread ×{1}.
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-mycelium-for: Near mycelium zone ({0} blocks): forest spread ×{1}.
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-mycelium-unr: Mycelium BE present but not registered yet.
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-mycelium-no-: No mycelium block entity under this cap — ecology data from mushroom type only.
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-mycelium-sur: Anchor niche: stressed (wrong forest / no tree host).
ecosystemflora:inspect-line-mycelium-fro: Network edge: yes — can spread outward.
Network edge: no — interior of mat.
ecosystemflora:inspect-survival-fail-no-: Climate survival: no solid ground below.
ecosystemflora:inspect-survival-fail-soi: Climate survival: soil type mismatch (here: {0}; needs one of: {1}).
Climate survival: soil not fertile enough ({0} < {1}).
ecosystemflora:inspect-survival-fail-for: Climate survival: local forest cover too sparse ({0} < {1}).
Climate survival: local forest cover too dense ({0} > {1}).
